messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0110",
  "title": "策略手游投放趋势观察",
  "page_index": 55,
  "page_range": "55-56"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0111",
    "title": "策略手游 热门创意策略",
    "page_range": "56-57",
    "summary": "This section provides a data-driven overview of 2025 advertising creative strategies for strategy mobile games, answering questions related to:\n- **Topics & Entities:** Strategy mobile game user acquisition (买量), SLG, RTS, tower defense, building games, casual SLG, roguelike RPG, wuxia MMO, mild-horror MMO, and the analytics platform 广大大 (GuangdaDa).\n- **Dates:** January 2025 through December 2025.\n- **Creative Procedures/Formulas:** Standard ad narrative structures, specifically: high-altitude fall → number growth/choice gate parkour → smooth mid-level clearance → intentional failure at the end.\n- **Claims & Trends:** Number gate parkour is becoming the primary UA method for casual SLG; traditional number-variable formats are being replaced by three-lane tracks, new items, and moving choice gates. Traditional numerical/IP progression, idle, roguelike RPG, wuxia, and mild-horror MMO formats currently hold mainstream audio-visual promotional advantages.\n- **Figures & Metrics:** Two monthly trend charts (Jan–Dec 2025) detailing: (1) Advertiser volume (4.0K–5.5K) and the ratio of advertisers launching new creatives (77.2%–89.3%); (2) Monthly average active creatives (0–400) and the ratio of new creatives (53.0%–65.8%).\n- **Evidence Types:** Platform-derived analytics from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, supported by statistical trend visualizations and comparative genre performance data.\n- **Retrieval Focus:** Optimized for queries on 2025 mobile game ad creative formulas, SLG/RTS UA strategy evolution, monthly advertiser/creative volume benchmarks, and genre-specific promotional performance metrics."
  }
]

message.content: This section (pages 55–56) provides a data-driven analysis of 2025 advertising and user acquisition (UA) trends for strategy mobile games, leveraging analytics from the 广大大 (GuangdaDa) platform. It covers core genres including SLG, RTS, tower defense, building games, casual SLG, roguelike RPG, wuxia MMO, and mild-horror MMO across January–December 2025. The content outlines standardized ad creative procedures, specifically a narrative formula: high-altitude fall → number growth/choice gate parkour → smooth mid-level clearance → intentional failure. Key claims highlight that number gate parkour has emerged as the primary UA method for casual SLG, with traditional number-variable formats being replaced by three-lane tracks, new items, and moving choice gates. Traditional numerical/IP progression, idle, roguelike RPG, wuxia, and mild-horror MMO formats currently retain mainstream audio-visual promotional advantages. The section features two monthly trend figures (Jan–Dec 2025) detailing advertiser volume (4.0K–5.5K) and new creative launch ratios (77.2%–89.3%), alongside monthly average active creatives (0–400) and new creative ratios (53.0%–65.8%). Evidence is sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, supported by statistical trend visualizations and comparative genre performance data. Optimized for retrieval on 2025 mobile game ad creative formulas, SLG/RTS UA strategy evolution, monthly advertiser/creative volume benchmarks, and genre-specific promotional metrics.
